classes.label: Agent
classes.description: A class representing an agent that is responsible for carrying out a process, producing data, or contributing to the provenance of a resource in H2KG.

classes.label: Data
classes.description: A class representing data produced, used, or referenced by a process or measurement in H2KG.

classes.label: Data Point
classes.description: A class representing a single data point that links a value to the property and measurement context it belongs to in H2KG.

classes.label: Instrument
classes.description: A class representing an instrument used to perform, support, or control a measurement or manufacturing process in H2KG.

classes.label: Manufacturing
classes.description: A class representing a manufacturing process that prepares, assembles, coats, or otherwise fabricates a material or device in H2KG.

classes.label: Matter
classes.description: A class representing a material entity that participates in a process, measurement, or device configuration in H2KG.

classes.label: Measurement
classes.description: A class representing a measurement process that determines one or more properties of a material, device, or system in H2KG.

classes.label: Metadata
classes.description: A class representing metadata used to describe the provenance, identity, structure, or context of another resource in H2KG.

classes.label: Parameter
classes.description: A class representing a parameter specified as an input, setting, or condition for a process or measurement in H2KG.

classes.label: Process
classes.description: A class representing a process that transforms material, produces data, or changes the state of a system in H2KG.

classes.label: Property
classes.description: A class representing a property that can be measured, computed, or otherwise assigned to a material, process, or system in H2KG.

classes.label: Unit
classes.description: A class representing a unit used to express the value of a quantity in H2KG.

properties.label: atCurrentDensity
properties.description: An object property relating a data point to the current-density value at which it is reported.

properties.label: fromMeasurement
properties.description: An object property relating a data point to the measurement from which it was generated.

properties.label: hasIdentifier
properties.description: A datatype property relating a resource to a literal identifier used to distinguish or reference it.

properties.label: hasInputData
properties.description: An object property relating a process or measurement to data used as an input.

properties.label: hasInputMaterial
properties.description: An object property relating a process or measurement to a material entity that serves as an input.

properties.label: hasOutputData
properties.description: An object property relating a measurement or process to data generated as an output.

properties.label: hasOutputMaterial
properties.description: An object property relating a process to a material entity produced as an output.

properties.label: hasParameter
properties.description: An object property relating a process or measurement to a parameter that specifies one of its inputs, settings, or operating conditions.

properties.label: hasPart
properties.description: An object property relating an entity or process to a constituent part.

properties.label: hasProperty
properties.description: An object property relating a measurement to a property that is determined, reported, or otherwise associated with that measurement.

properties.label: hasQuantityValue
properties.description: An object property relating a parameter, property, or data point to the quantity value that expresses it.

properties.label: hasSubProcess
properties.description: An object property relating a process to a subprocess that forms part of its overall execution.

properties.label: isPartOf
properties.description: An object property relating an entity or process to a larger entity or process of which it forms a part.

properties.label: isSubProcessOf
properties.description: An object property relating a subprocess to the larger process of which it forms a part.

properties.label: measures
properties.description: An object property relating a measurement to a property that it determines.

properties.label: normalizedTo
properties.description: An object property relating a property or result to the basis used for its normalization.

properties.label: ofProperty
properties.description: An object property relating a data point to the property represented by that data point.

properties.label: referenceElectrode
properties.description: An object property relating an electrochemical measurement setup or process to the reference electrode it uses.

properties.label: usesInstrument
properties.description: An object property relating a process or measurement to an instrument used to carry it out or support it.

examples.label: ACAmplitude
examples.description: A parameter specifying acamplitude used to characterize Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Potentiostatic and Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Galvanostatic.

examples.label: Bias Potential
examples.description: A parameter specifying bias potential used to characterize Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Potentiostatic, Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry, and Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Galvanostatic.

examples.label: Catalyst Impregnation
examples.description: A manufacturing process for catalyst impregnation using Support Material and Precursor Solution with Oven and producing Impregnated Support.

examples.label: Catalyst Layer Porosity
examples.description: A parameter specifying catalyst layer porosity used to characterize PEMFCCFD Simulation, Electrochemical Reaction Simulation, and Catalyst Layer Composition Specification.

examples.label: Catalyst Layer Thickness
examples.description: A parameter specifying catalyst layer thickness used to characterize Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Galvanostatic, Transmission Line Model Analysis, and PEMFCCFD Simulation.

examples.label: Catalyst Layer Tortuosity
examples.description: A parameter specifying catalyst layer tortuosity.

examples.label: Catalyst Layer Volume
examples.description: A normalization basis corresponding to catalyst layer volume and used as the reference basis for reporting quantitative results or properties.

examples.label: Cathode Stoichiometric Ratio
examples.description: A parameter specifying cathode stoichiometric ratio used to characterize MEAPolarization, Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Galvanostatic, and Break In Procedure.

examples.label: Compression Pressure
examples.description: A parameter specifying compression pressure used to characterize MEAAssembly Process and Through Plane Thermal Conductivity Measurement.

examples.label: Current Density
examples.description: A property denoting current density normalized to Geometric Area and determined in MEAPolarization, Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry, and Cyclic Voltammetry.

examples.label: Current-Interrupt HFR Measurement
examples.description: A measurement process that uses Current-Interrupt HFR measurement to determine Ohmic Resistance and High Frequency Resistance using Fuel Cell Test Station and Milli Ohmmeter and generating Experiment Dataset.

examples.label: Dynamic Hydrogen Electrode (H2 Anode in MEA)
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a dynamic hydrogen electrode and used as a reference electrode in electrochemical measurements.

examples.label: ECSA Basis
examples.description: A normalization basis corresponding to ECSA basis and used as the reference basis for reporting quantitative results or properties.

examples.label: Effective Ionic Conductivity
examples.description: A property denoting effective ionic conductivity and determined in Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Potentiostatic, Proton Conductivity Measurement, and Literature Review Synthesis.

examples.label: Effective Oxygen Diffusion Coefficient
examples.description: A property denoting effective oxygen diffusion coefficient and determined in Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement, X Ray Computed Tomography Imaging, and Distribution Of Relaxation Times Analysis.

examples.label: Environmental Chamber
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to an environmental chamber and used in Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement.

examples.label: Flow Field Design
examples.description: A parameter specifying flow field design.

examples.label: Frequency
examples.description: A parameter specifying frequency used to characterize Current-Interrupt HFR Measurement, Square Wave Voltammetry, and Spray Coating.

examples.label: GDE Fixture
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a GDE fixture.

examples.label: GDL Type
examples.description: A parameter specifying GDL type used to characterize MEAPolarization.

examples.label: Gas Composition
examples.description: A parameter specifying gas composition used to characterize Oxygen Gain/Loss Measurement, O2–N2 Switching Measurement, and Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement.

examples.label: Gas Humidification Unit
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a gas humidification unit and used in Oxygen Gain/Loss Measurement, O2–N2 Switching Measurement, and MEAPolarization.

examples.label: Geometric Area
examples.description: A normalization basis corresponding to geometric area and used as the reference basis for reporting quantitative results or properties.

examples.label: Ink Aging Time
examples.description: A parameter specifying ink aging time used to characterize Catalyst Ink Preparation and Dynamic Light Scattering Measurement.

examples.label: Ionomer Equivalent Weight
examples.description: A parameter specifying ionomer equivalent weight used to characterize Catalyst Ink Preparation, Discrete Element Method Packing Simulation, and Ionomer Coating Simulation.

examples.label: Ionomer Mass
examples.description: A normalization basis corresponding to ionomer mass and used as the reference basis for reporting quantitative results or properties.

examples.label: Kinetic Current
examples.description: A property denoting kinetic current normalized to Geometric Area and determined in Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry and Linear Sweep Voltammetry.

examples.label: Limiting Current Density
examples.description: A property denoting limiting current density normalized to Geometric Area and determined in Oxygen Gain/Loss Measurement, Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry, and Linear Sweep Voltammetry.

examples.label: Local Oxygen Transport Resistance
examples.description: A property denoting local oxygen transport resistance normalized to Geometric Area and determined in Oxygen Gain/Loss Measurement, O2–N2 Switching Measurement, and Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement.

examples.label: MEA Polarization
examples.description: A measurement process that records membrane electrode assembly polarization behavior to determine current density, power density, mass activity, and related electrochemical performance properties under controlled oper...

examples.label: Mass Flow Controller
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a mass flow controller and used in MEAPolarization, Spray Coating, and Fuel Cell Test Station.

examples.label: Mass Transport Resistance
examples.description: A property denoting mass transport resistance normalized to Geometric Area and determined in Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement, MEAPolarization, and Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Potentiostatic.

examples.label: Membrane Type
examples.description: A parameter specifying membrane type used to characterize MEAAssembly Process.

examples.label: Microporous Layer Presence
examples.description: A parameter specifying microporous layer presence.

examples.label: O2–N2 Switching Measurement
examples.description: A measurement process that uses O2–N2 switching measurement to determine Local Oxygen Transport Resistance using Fuel Cell Test Station and Gas Humidification Unit and generating Experiment Dataset.

examples.label: Ohmic Resistance
examples.description: A property denoting ohmic resistance and determined in Current-Interrupt HFR Measurement, MEAPolarization, and Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Potentiostatic.

examples.label: Onset Potential
examples.description: A property denoting onset potential and determined in Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry, Cyclic Voltammetry, and Linear Sweep Voltammetry.

examples.label: Operating Temperature
examples.description: A parameter specifying operating temperature used to characterize Oxygen Gain/Loss Measurement, O2–N2 Switching Measurement, and Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement.

examples.label: Oven
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to an oven and used in Catalyst Impregnation, Convection Drying, and Vacuum Drying.

examples.label: Overpotential
examples.description: A property denoting overpotential and determined in MEAPolarization, Linear Sweep Voltammetry, and Electrochemical Reaction Simulation.

examples.label: Oxygen Gain/Loss Measurement
examples.description: A measurement process that uses oxygen Gain/Loss measurement to determine Local Oxygen Transport Resistance, Limiting Current Density, and Local Gas Transport Resistance using Fuel Cell Test Station and Gas Humidifica...

examples.label: Peak Potential
examples.description: A property denoting peak potential and determined in Square Wave Voltammetry, Cyclic Voltammetry, and Cathodic Stripping Voltammetry.

examples.label: Precursor Concentration
examples.description: A parameter specifying precursor concentration used to characterize Catalyst Impregnation, Catalyst Ink Preparation, and Dip Coating Process.

examples.label: Precursor Solution
examples.description: A material entity corresponding to a precursor solution and used as an input in Catalyst Impregnation.

examples.label: Protonated Membrane
examples.description: A material entity corresponding to a protonated membrane and used as an input in MEAAssembly Process.

examples.label: Pt Mass
examples.description: A normalization basis corresponding to pt mass and used as the reference basis for reporting quantitative results or properties.

examples.label: RRDE Assembly
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a rotating ring-disk electrode assembly and used in rotating ring-disk voltammetry and related electrochemical measurements.

examples.label: Ring Collection Efficiency
examples.description: A parameter specifying ring collection efficiency used to characterize Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry.

examples.label: Rotation Rate
examples.description: A parameter specifying rotation rate used to characterize Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Potentiostatic, Rotating Ring Disk Voltammetry, and Cyclic Voltammetry.

examples.label: SCE
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a saturated calomel electrode and used as a reference electrode in electrochemical measurements.

examples.label: Solid Content Fraction
examples.description: A parameter specifying solid content fraction used to characterize Catalyst Ink Preparation, Dynamic Light Scattering Measurement, and Rheology Measurement.

examples.label: Sonication Power
examples.description: A parameter specifying sonication power used to characterize Ink Ultrasonication, Ultrasonic Liquid Exfoliation, and Ultrasonic Deposition Process.

examples.label: Sonication Time
examples.description: A parameter specifying sonication time used to characterize Catalyst Ink Preparation, Ink Ultrasonication, and Analytical Centrifuge Sedimentation Measurement.

examples.label: Spray Coating
examples.description: Robotic spray deposition of ink in multiple passes onto heated substrate to evaporate solvent between passes.

examples.label: Square Wave Voltammetry
examples.description: A measurement process that uses square wave voltammetry to determine Peak Current and Peak Potential using Electrochemical Workstation and generating Experiment Dataset.

examples.label: Stirring Time
examples.description: A parameter specifying duration of stirring during synthesis/sol-gel modification.

examples.label: Tafel Slope
examples.description: A property denoting tafel slope and determined in MEAPolarization, Linear Sweep Voltammetry, and Tafel Slope Step Analysis.

examples.label: Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement
examples.description: A measurement process that uses Through-Plane gas transport measurement to determine Local Oxygen Transport Resistance, Effective Oxygen Diffusion Coefficient, and Mass Transport Resistance using Through-Plane Transpo...

examples.label: Through-Plane Transport Cell
examples.description: An instrument corresponding to a Through-Plane transport cell and used in Through-Plane Gas Transport Measurement and Through Plane Thermal Conductivity Measurement.

examples.label: Water Uptake Indicator
examples.description: A property denoting water uptake indicator and determined in Water Vapor Adsorption Measurement, Literature Review Synthesis, and Molecular Dynamics Simulation.

mappings.rationale: Agent should remain local while anchored in PROV-O.
Carbon dioxide is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Ethanol is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Formic acid is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Hydrazine is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Hydrochloric acid is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Hydrofluoric acid is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Measurement is better treated as a local specialization of electrochemical measurement practice.
Potassium hydroxide is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
High lexical similarity and compatible kind support a strong equivalence proposal.
High lexical similarity and compatible kind support a strong equivalence proposal.
Water is an unambiguous chemical entity that should align directly to ChEBI.
Conservative anchoring favors specialization over strict equivalence.
Quantity-value relations should reuse QUDT semantics where possible.
